description: Tempdoc 560 §4b/§5 LIVE-REGISTRY witness register (ADR-0042). Consumer-presence has three tiers: the static `consumer-presence` gate (a declaration NAMES a consumer); the static `runtime-witness` gate (AGENT-channel — declared agent consumers match what AgentOperationEmitter would deliver, from the static catalogs); and THIS live-registry witness, the only tier that examines the running ContributionRegistry, so it covers the contributions composed at RUNTIME that both static tiers are blind to (the DR-D class, e.g. core.workflow-* ops, MCP tools, plugins). The teeth are the backend live-registry test (DR-A: an offline gate cannot see runtime composition); this register + the check-live-witness early-warning keep the authority from being silently removed or forked (mirrors the tempdoc 575 in-flight-liveness register).

complements.note: The existing `runtime-witness` discipline gate checks the AGENT delivery channel over the STATIC snapshot. This live-witness is its live-registry complement: same §5 keystone, the tier that sees runtime composition. Not a fork — a different tier of the one consumer-presence notion.

authority.note: The ONE live-registry consumer-presence authority. Do not re-implement the live consumer-presence check per-surface; derive from this.

reuses.note: The live-registry witness reuses the build-tier consumer merge (inline ConsumerHooks union executor-derived) verbatim — it is NOT a second/forked authority, just the same notion applied over the live set.

scope.note: ConsumerDeclaring kinds only. Surfaces/diagnostic-channels/conversation-shapes are consumers, not consumer-declaring, so they are out of scope.

decisions._doc: The four §E.2.1 decisions resolved as defensible defaults — see docs/decisions/0042-runtime-witness-consumer-presence.md.
decisions.agentConsumerWitnessShape: Attest at the DELIVERY SEAM, not consumption. An agent tool's consumer is satisfied by being bound into the live toolset; delivery != invocation.
decisions.perActorDeadline: None. Attested-at-compose, tier-keyed: lockstep core/FE/agent at boot, independent plugin/MCP on install(Installation). 'Undelivered' = declared-but-never-composed (a live absence), not a wall-clock timeout.
decisions.sliceCatalogIntegrity: Out of scope. Realized hooks only; a future Promised graduation stays a separate substrate primitive.
decisions.trafficCounter: Delivery-PRESENCE, not a count. Reuses the live registry / witness; no new RegistryEntry-shaped TrafficCounter primitive.
